title: Setting Verilated::gotFinish() to true without $finish
body: I am writing C++ code that when Verilated with a given Verilog file, exchanges inputs and outputs for the top module in said Verilog file with a script using stdin/stdout.  The Verilog file simply describes some form of hardware (simple stuff like shift registers and counters) and has no testbench code that will call $finish.  In my main.cpp file where I instantiate the top module using Vtop* top = new Vtop, how would I cause the simulation loop to correctly break with Verilated::gotFinish() when certain conditions are met (i.e. invalid input is received from the script, 5 minutes have passed without activity etc.)?
